summary: Regulatory oversight for research involving genetically engineered organisms, hazardous microorganisms, and recombinant DNA.
registration: Registered and accredited under DBT (Department of Biotechnology), Ministry of Science and Technology, Government of India — RIIDL Foundation IBSC

regulatory_framework.governing_rules: Rules for Manufacture, Use, Import, Export and Storage of Hazardous Microorganisms, Genetically Engineered Organisms or Cells, 1989 (Rules 1989) under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986 (MoEF&CC)
regulatory_framework.scope: Covers manufacture, import, research, and release of Genetically Engineered Organisms (GEOs) and hazardous microorganisms
responsibilities: Review and grant clearance for restricted-category GMO, LMO, and rDNA research proposals
Formulate biosafety programs and conduct onsite evaluations
Monitor research facilities for adherence to biosafety guidelines
Oversee regulatory process, update PIs on approvals/rejections
Assess field experiments and review emergency plans

committee_members.name: Dr Jyotirlatha Bangera
committee_members.affiliation: Professor & Head of Department, K. J. Somaiya Medical College

committee_members.name: Dr Maithili Athavale
committee_members.affiliation: Assistant General Manager R&D, Godavari Biorefineries

committee_members.name: Dr Kafil Ahmed
committee_members.affiliation: Chief Scientific Officer, Techinvention Lifecare Pvt Ltd

steps: Download the application form from the IBSC Protocol Form link on the website
Submit hard copy to Member Secretary, RIIDL Foundation, and soft copy via email to bhavna.pandya@somaiya.edu (CC: apekshakumari@somaiya.edu)
Submit at least 30 days prior to scheduled IBSC meetings
Principal Investigators must inform IBSC of rDNA, GMO, or LMO work before applying for external grants
selection_process: IBSC schedules a meeting and informs applicant at least 15 days in advance
Applicant presents the proposal during the IBSC meeting
Decision communicated within one week after the meeting
